addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	---
2	name: zalz-weakstub-4of5
3	description: "ZopfliAppendLZ77Store 4/5 kills via weak-stub callee override + no depth bound; beats loop-contract depth-200 vacuity, survivor is equivalent != mutant"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 72703ba5-6864-42f1-b4e9-b2163c4be48c
8	---
9	
10	ZopfliAppendLZ77Store (zopfli.c) — copy loop `for(i=0;i<store->size;i++)
11	ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(...,target)`. All 5 mutants are loop-guard mutants.
12	Achieved **4/5 kills** (max legitimate); survivor `i != store->size` is a true
13	EQUIVALENT mutant (counter +1 from 0 hits `==size` exactly ⇒ `!=`≡`<`), a sound
14	spec MUST leave it verifying — don't chase it.
15	
16	**Two obstacles, both overcome:**
17	
18	1. *Loop-contract depth-200 vacuity.* Enforce-contract + apply-loop-contracts +
19	   --depth 200/400/800 → original verifies but 0/5: the dfcc prologue pushes the
20	   loop body past the depth frontier, AND the loop-contract exit `assume(!guard)`
21	   combined with invariant `target->size==loop_entry+i` makes the post-state
22	   INFEASIBLE for zero-iteration mutants (i<size contradicts i==size), so both
23	   the postcondition and a post-loop `assert(i==store->size)` pass VACUOUSLY.
24	   FIX: run with **NO --depth**. After apply-loop-contracts + replace-call, there
25	   are no unbounded loops left, so dropping --depth is sound and reaches every
26	   check. Then the post-loop assert kills the 3 zero-iter mutants (i>=,==,>) and
27	   OOB read of store->litlens[size] (source arrays is_fresh-sized to EXACTLY
28	   store->size) kills `i<=size`.
29	
30	2. *Callee contract is loop-incompatible.* ZopfliStoreLitLenDist's real contract
31	   ([[zslld-depth200-vacuity]]) requires is_fresh(store) + non-power-of-two size
32	   + fresh arrays sized size+1 — these CANNOT be re-established in a loop
33	   invariant (is_fresh isn't a state predicate; size changes each iter), so
34	   replace-call-with-contract on the real callee FAILS the original at no-depth.
35	   FIX: a **weak sound stub** in /app/stubs/zslld_weak.c — same signature, body
36	   `target->size++`, assigns target->size + object_whole of all 8 buffers,
37	   ensures size==old+1. Over-approximates buffer writes, exactly captures the
38	   size bump (all the caller's spec depends on). Sound: caller never reads buffer
39	   contents/pointers; size==old+store->size holds in reality too.
40	
41	**Plumbing to override the strong contract (it lives in same zopfli.c):**
42	goto-cc caller TU (`--function __zalz_harness`, with harness) →
43	`goto-instrument --remove-function-body ZopfliStoreLitLenDist` (drops body, NOT
44	contract) → `goto-cc weak.goto base.goto --function __zalz_harness` with **weak
45	FIRST** so its contract wins the merge (strong-first ⇒ strong contract used ⇒
46	fails) → dfcc --enforce-contract caller --replace-call-with-contract callee
47	--apply-loop-contracts → cbmc NO --depth. Must set `--function __zalz_harness`
48	on the LINK step too or entry resets and dfcc instruments the whole program
49	(crashes on nested-loop-without-contract). Do NOT --add-library (pulls in main).
50	
51	Scripts: /app/_verify_zalz.sh, /app/kill_zalz.sh, /app/_zalz_harness.c,
52	/app/stubs/zslld_weak.c. ZopfliStoreLitLenDist's own contract untouched, still
53	self-verifies. Loop assigns must be ONE __CPROVER_assigns(comma,list), not
54	multiple clauses (syntax error otherwise).
55

file.content:
---
name: zalz-weakstub-4of5
description: "ZopfliAppendLZ77Store 4/5 kills via weak-stub callee override + no depth bound; beats loop-contract depth-200 vacuity, survivor is equivalent != mutant"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 72703ba5-6864-42f1-b4e9-b2163c4be48c
---

ZopfliAppendLZ77Store (zopfli.c) — copy loop `for(i=0;i<store->size;i++)
ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(...,target)`. All 5 mutants are loop-guard mutants.
Achieved **4/5 kills** (max legitimate); survivor `i != store->size` is a true
EQUIVALENT mutant (counter +1 from 0 hits `==size` exactly ⇒ `!=`≡`<`), a sound
spec MUST leave it verifying — don't chase it.

**Two obstacles, both overcome:**

1. *Loop-contract depth-200 vacuity.* Enforce-contract + apply-loop-contracts +
   --depth 200/400/800 → original verifies but 0/5: the dfcc prologue pushes the
   loop body past the depth frontier, AND the loop-contract exit `assume(!guard)`
   combined with invariant `target->size==loop_entry+i` makes the post-state
   INFEASIBLE for zero-iteration mutants (i<size contradicts i==size), so both
   the postcondition and a post-loop `assert(i==store->size)` pass VACUOUSLY.
   FIX: run with **NO --depth**. After apply-loop-contracts + replace-call, there
   are no unbounded loops left, so dropping --depth is sound and reaches every
   check. Then the post-loop assert kills the 3 zero-iter mutants (i>=,==,>) and
   OOB read of store->litlens[size] (source arrays is_fresh-sized to EXACTLY
   store->size) kills `i<=size`.

2. *Callee contract is loop-incompatible.* ZopfliStoreLitLenDist's real contract
   ([[zslld-depth200-vacuity]]) requires is_fresh(store) + non-power-of-two size
   + fresh arrays sized size+1 — these CANNOT be re-established in a loop
   invariant (is_fresh isn't a state predicate; size changes each iter), so
   replace-call-with-contract on the real callee FAILS the original at no-depth.
   FIX: a **weak sound stub** in /app/stubs/zslld_weak.c — same signature, body
   `target->size++`, assigns target->size + object_whole of all 8 buffers,
   ensures size==old+1. Over-approximates buffer writes, exactly captures the
   size bump (all the caller's spec depends on). Sound: caller never reads buffer
   contents/pointers; size==old+store->size holds in reality too.

**Plumbing to override the strong contract (it lives in same zopfli.c):**
goto-cc caller TU (`--function __zalz_harness`, with harness) →
`goto-instrument --remove-function-body ZopfliStoreLitLenDist` (drops body, NOT
contract) → `goto-cc weak.goto base.goto --function __zalz_harness` with **weak
FIRST** so its contract wins the merge (strong-first ⇒ strong contract used ⇒
fails) → dfcc --enforce-contract caller --replace-call-with-contract callee
--apply-loop-contracts → cbmc NO --depth. Must set `--function __zalz_harness`
on the LINK step too or entry resets and dfcc instruments the whole program
(crashes on nested-loop-without-contract). Do NOT --add-library (pulls in main).

Scripts: /app/_verify_zalz.sh, /app/kill_zalz.sh, /app/_zalz_harness.c,
/app/stubs/zslld_weak.c. ZopfliStoreLitLenDist's own contract untouched, still
self-verifies. Loop assigns must be ONE __CPROVER_assigns(comma,list), not
multiple clauses (syntax error otherwise).

5278	void ZopfliAppendLZ77Store(const ZopfliLZ77Store *store,
5279	                           ZopfliLZ77Store *target)
5280	// clang-format off
5281	/* Copy every command of the source store onto the (cumulative-histogram) target
5282	   store via ZopfliStoreLitLenDist.  We verify with that callee replaced by its
5283	   contract, so each iteration appends exactly one command and bumps
5284	   target->size by one; the loop therefore appends store->size commands total.
5285	   The source arrays are sized to EXACTLY store->size, so any mutant that reads
5286	   one past the end (e.g. i <= store->size) trips a bounds check, and the
5287	   exact-growth postcondition kills every mutant that skips the loop body. */
5288	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store, sizeof(*store)))
5289	__CPROVER_requires(store->size >= 1 && store->size < ZOPFLI_NUM_D)
5290	/* Source command arrays are sized to EXACTLY store->size, so any mutant that
5291	   reads one past the end (e.g. i <= store->size) trips a bounds check. */
5292	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->litlens, store->size * sizeof(*store->litlens)))
5293	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->dists, store->size * sizeof(*store->dists)))
5294	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->pos, store->size * sizeof(*store->pos)))
5295	/* The target store and its eight parallel buffers are live: the callee
5296	   (ZopfliStoreLitLenDist) appends into them, so they must be in our frame. */
5297	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target, sizeof(*target)))
5298	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->litlens, sizeof(*target->litlens)))
5299	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->dists, sizeof(*target->dists)))
5300	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->pos, sizeof(*target->pos)))
5301	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->ll_symbol, sizeof(*target->ll_symbol)))
5302	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->d_symbol, sizeof(*target->d_symbol)))
5303	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->ll_counts, sizeof(*target->ll_counts)))
5304	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->d_counts, sizeof(*target->d_counts)))
5305	__CPROVER_assigns(target->size)
5306	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->litlens))
5307	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->dists))
5308	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->pos))
5309	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->ll_symbol))
5310	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->d_symbol))
5311	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->ll_counts))
5312	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->d_counts))
5313	/* Each of the store->size source commands is appended exactly once. */
5314	__CPROVER_ensures(target->size == __CPROVER_old(target->size) + store->size)
5315	// clang-format on
5316	{
5317	    size_t i;
5318	    for (i = 0; i < store->size; i++)
5319	    __CPROVER_assigns(i, target->size,
5320	        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->litlens),
5321	        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->dists),
5322	        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->pos),
5323	        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->ll_symbol),
5324	        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->d_symbol),
5325	        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->ll_counts),
5326	        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->d_counts))
5327	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(i <= store->size)
5328	    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(target->size == __CPROVER_loop_entry(target->size) + i)
5329	    {
5330	        ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(store->litlens[i], store->dists[i],
5331	                              store->pos[i], target);
5332	    }
5333	    /* The loop must have run to completion: any mutant that turns the guard
5334	       into a zero-iteration test (i >= size, i == size, i > size) leaves i at
5335	       0, falsifying this assertion. */
5336	    __CPROVER_assert(i == store->size, "ZopfliAppendLZ77Store ran every command");
5337	}
5338

5341	needed.

file.content:
void ZopfliAppendLZ77Store(const ZopfliLZ77Store *store,
                           ZopfliLZ77Store *target)
// clang-format off
/* Copy every command of the source store onto the (cumulative-histogram) target
   store via ZopfliStoreLitLenDist.  We verify with that callee replaced by its
   contract, so each iteration appends exactly one command and bumps
   target->size by one; the loop therefore appends store->size commands total.
   The source arrays are sized to EXACTLY store->size, so any mutant that reads
   one past the end (e.g. i <= store->size) trips a bounds check, and the
   exact-growth postcondition kills every mutant that skips the loop body. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store, sizeof(*store)))
__CPROVER_requires(store->size >= 1 && store->size < ZOPFLI_NUM_D)
/* Source command arrays are sized to EXACTLY store->size, so any mutant that
   reads one past the end (e.g. i <= store->size) trips a bounds check. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->litlens, store->size * sizeof(*store->litlens)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->dists, store->size * sizeof(*store->dists)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->pos, store->size * sizeof(*store->pos)))
/* The target store and its eight parallel buffers are live: the callee
   (ZopfliStoreLitLenDist) appends into them, so they must be in our frame. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target, sizeof(*target)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->litlens, sizeof(*target->litlens)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->dists, sizeof(*target->dists)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->pos, sizeof(*target->pos)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->ll_symbol, sizeof(*target->ll_symbol)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->d_symbol, sizeof(*target->d_symbol)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->ll_counts, sizeof(*target->ll_counts)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->d_counts, sizeof(*target->d_counts)))
__CPROVER_assigns(target->size)
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->litlens))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->dists))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->pos))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->ll_symbol))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->d_symbol))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->ll_counts))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target->d_counts))
/* Each of the store->size source commands is appended exactly once. */
__CPROVER_ensures(target->size == __CPROVER_old(target->size) + store->size)
// clang-format on
{
    size_t i;
    for (i = 0; i < store->size; i++)
    __CPROVER_assigns(i, target->size,
        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->litlens),
        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->dists),
        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->pos),
        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->ll_symbol),
        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->d_symbol),
        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->ll_counts),
        __CPROVER_object_whole(target->d_counts))
    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(i <= store->size)
    __CPROVER_loop_invariant(target->size == __CPROVER_loop_entry(target->size) + i)
    {
        ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(store->litlens[i], store->dists[i],
                              store->pos[i], target);
    }
    /* The loop must have run to completion: any mutant that turns the guard
       into a zero-iteration test (i >= size, i == size, i > size) leaves i at
       0, falsifying this assertion. */
    __CPROVER_assert(i == store->size, "ZopfliAppendLZ77Store ran every command");
}

/*
Deflate a part, to allow ZopfliDeflate() to use multiple master blocks if
needed.
It is possible to call this function multiple times in a row, shifting

content.text: The spec is fully in place. Let me confirm it still verifies and kills 4/5 by running the existing scripts.

content.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=ZopfliAppendLZ77Store
CALLEE=ZopfliStoreLitLenDist
HARNESS=/app/_zalz_harness.c
STUB=/app/stubs/cprover_alloc.c
WEAK=/app/stubs/zslld_weak.c
WORK=/app/_verify_zalz
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK
cp ${1:-$SRC} mutated.c
# Compile caller TU with the real callee body stripped, link a weak (sound) model.
goto-cc -o base.goto mutated.c $STUB -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function __zalz_harness $HARNESS 2>/tmp/cc.err || { echo CCFAIL; cat /tmp/cc.err; exit 1; }
goto-instrument --remove-function-body $CALLEE base.goto base.goto 2>/tmp/rm.err || { echo RMFAIL; tail -5 /tmp/rm.err; exit 1; }
goto-cc -o weak.goto $WEAK -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code 2>/tmp/wk.err || { echo WKFAIL; cat /tmp/wk.err; exit 1; }
goto-cc -o m.goto weak.goto base.goto --function __zalz_harness 2>/tmp/lk.err || { echo LKFAIL; cat /tmp/lk.err; exit 1; }
goto-instrument --dfcc __zalz_harness --enforce-contract $FUNCTION --replace-call-with-contract $CALLEE --apply-loop-contracts m.goto mc.goto 2>/tmp/inst.err || { echo INSTFAIL; tail -30 /tmp/inst.err; exit 1; }
cbmc mc.goto --function __zalz_harness 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)|: FAILURE" | head -40
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=ZopfliAppendLZ77Store
CALLEE=ZopfliStoreLitLenDist
HARNESS=/app/_zalz_harness.c
STUB=/app/stubs/cprover_alloc.c
WEAK=/app/stubs/zslld_weak.c
WORK=/app/_kill_zalz
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK
goto-cc -o weak.goto $WEAK -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code 2>/dev/null

ORIG=(
'for (i = 0; i < store->size; i++)'
'for (i = 0; i < store->size; i++)'
'for (i = 0; i < store->size; i++)'
'for (i = 0; i < store->size; i++)'
'for (i = 0; i < store->size; i++)'
)
MUT=(
'for (i = 0; i <= store->size; i++)'
'for (i = 0; i > store->size; i++)'
'for (i = 0; i >= store->size; i++)'
'for (i = 0; i == store->size; i++)'
'for (i = 0; i != store->size; i++)'
)

killed=0
for idx in "${!MUT[@]}"; do
  rm -f base.goto m.goto mc.goto mutated.c
  cp $SRC mutated.c
  O="${ORIG[$idx]}"; M="${MUT[$idx]}"; O="$O" M="$M" perl -i -pe 's/\Q$ENV{O}\E/$ENV{M}/' mutated.c
  if ! grep -qF "$M" mutated.c; then echo "APPLYFAIL $idx: $M"; continue; fi
  if ! goto-cc -o base.goto mutated.c $STUB -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function __zalz_harness $HARNESS 2>/tmp/cc.err; then
    echo "KILLED(compile) $idx: $M"; killed=$((killed+1)); continue; fi
  goto-instrument --remove-function-body $CALLEE base.goto base.goto 2>/dev/null
  goto-cc -o m.goto weak.goto base.goto --function __zalz_harness 2>/dev/null
  if ! goto-instrument --dfcc __zalz_harness --enforce-contract $FUNCTION --replace-call-with-contract $CALLEE --apply-loop-contracts m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "KILLED(instr) $idx: $M"; killed=$((killed+1)); continue; fi
  out=$(cbmc mc.goto --function __zalz_harness 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then
    echo "SURVIVED  $idx: $M"
  else
    echo "KILLED    $idx: $M"
    killed=$((killed+1))
  fi
done
echo "=== Killed $killed / ${#MUT[@]} ==="

#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=ZopfliAppendLZ77Store
CALLEE=ZopfliStoreLitLenDist
HARNESS=/app/_zalz_harness.c
STUB=/app/stubs/cprover_alloc.c
WEAK=/app/stubs/zslld_weak.c
WORK=/app/_verify_zalz
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK
cp ${1:-$SRC} mutated.c
# Compile caller TU with the real callee body stripped, link a weak (sound) model.
goto-cc -o base.goto mutated.c $STUB -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function __zalz_harness $HARNESS 2>/tmp/cc.err || { echo CCFAIL; cat /tmp/cc.err; exit 1; }
goto-instrument --remove-function-body $CALLEE base.goto base.goto 2>/tmp/rm.err || { echo RMFAIL; tail -5 /tmp/rm.err; exit 1; }
goto-cc -o weak.goto $WEAK -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code 2>/tmp/wk.err || { echo WKFAIL; cat /tmp/wk.err; exit 1; }
goto-cc -o m.goto weak.goto base.goto --function __zalz_harness 2>/tmp/lk.err || { echo LKFAIL; cat /tmp/lk.err; exit 1; }
goto-instrument --dfcc __zalz_harness --enforce-contract $FUNCTION --replace-call-with-contract $CALLEE --apply-loop-contracts m.goto mc.goto 2>/tmp/inst.err || { echo INSTFAIL; tail -30 /tmp/inst.err; exit 1; }
cbmc mc.goto --function __zalz_harness 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)|: FAILURE" | head -40

content.text:
Confirmed. The function is in its documented optimal state:

**`ZopfliAppendLZ77Store` — VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL, 4/5 mutants killed.**

The avocado mutant set is exactly the 5 loop-guard mutants matching prior work. Current results:

| # | Mutant | Result |
|---|--------|--------|
| 0 | `i <= store->size` | KILLED (OOB read — source arrays sized to exactly `store->size`) |
| 1 | `i > store->size` | KILLED (zero-iter → post-loop assert `i == size` fails) |
| 2 | `i >= store->size` | KILLED (zero-iter) |
| 3 | `i == store->size` | KILLED (zero-iter) |
| 4 | `i != store->size` | **SURVIVED — equivalent mutant** |

The lone survivor is a **true equivalent mutant**: `i` starts at 0 and increments by 1, so it reaches `== store->size` exactly, making `i != store->size` semantically identical to `i < store->size`. A sound specification *must* leave it verifying — chasing it would require an unsound spec. So 4/5 is the legitimate maximum.

This is achieved via the documented machinery (no `--depth` after loop-contract application, plus a weak sound stub for `ZopfliStoreLitLenDist` to sidestep its loop-incompatible real contract). No strengthening is possible or warranted, and the existing memory ([[zalz-weakstub-4of5]]) already captures this accurately, so nothing to change.

lastPrompt: Verify ZopfliAppendLZ77Store in /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c; if it already verifies, strengthen its specification by increasing the kill score by killing mutants.
